name: El Granado, Puebla de Guzmán, Villanueva de los Castillejos – circular route
name_original: El Granado, Puebla de Guzmán, Villanueva de los Castillejos – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around El Granado traverse a diverse landscape in the western part of Huelva province, Spain. The region features undulating terrain, farmlands, and forests, bordered by the Guadiana River. Cyclists encounter historical remnants such as the Puente del Lobo and an ancient Roman bridge, alongside natural features like the Chanza Dam. The area's "mining memory" also contributes to its unique visual character. Best road cycling routes around El Granado The most popular road cycling route is Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route, a 41.3 miles (66.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Granado, Puebla de Guzmán, Villanueva de los Castillejos – circular route, a moderate 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This route circulates through El Granado, Puebla de Guzmán, and Villanueva de los Castillejos, featuring an elevation gain of 447 meters. Local road cyclists also love the El Granado – Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos, a 35.5 miles (57.2 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around El Granado is defined by its undulating terrain, views of the Guadiana River, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in El Granado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Granado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Granado?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around El Granado, catering to various fitness levels. Most of these are moderate, with a few more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Granado?
A: Road cycling routes around El Granado feature diverse and undulating terrain. You'll encounter well-paved roads traversing farmlands, forests, and pastures, often with scenic views of the Guadiana River and historical elements.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in El Granado?
A: The region is rich in history. You might encounter remnants like the Puente del Lobo, an ancient Roman bridge, and a short tunnel carved through rock. The area's 'mining memory' also shapes its unique landscape. For specific historical sites, consider exploring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanlúcar de Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, a border town with historical significance.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer stunning panoramic views. The Guadiana River forms a significant natural boundary, and you'll find numerous viewpoints, particularly along the Guadiana Natural Trail, providing vistas of the river, the Ratilla riverbank, and even the neighboring Portuguese landscape. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4546209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in El Granado?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in El Granado are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991342727/villanueva-de-los-castillejos-puebla-de-guzman-tharsis-san-bartolome-de-la-torre-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route&lt;/a&gt; (66.5 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991346280/el-granado-puebla-de-guzman-villanueva-de-los-castillejos-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado, Puebla de Guzmán, Villanueva de los Castillejos – circular route&lt;/a&gt; (49.9 km).

Q: Can I cycle across the border into Portugal from El Granado?
A: Yes, El Granado's proximity to the Portuguese border makes cross-border cycling an exciting option. A notable route leads through Pomarão, climbs to Mertola, descends to Alcoutim, and then returns to El Granado, often involving a boat crossing at Sanlúcar del Guadiana.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, El Granado offers longer and more demanding routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado – View of the Guadiana River loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Granado?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in El Granado?
A: The road cycling routes in El Granado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the blend of natural beauty, historical sites, and the varied, undulating terrain that offers engaging rides.

Q: Are there any natural features to explore along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the Guadiana River, you can encounter the impressive Chanza Dam, one of the largest in the province, which adds to the characteristic scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7785262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Copper lakes of the mine&lt;/a&gt; also offer a unique natural and historical landscape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options, even if not strictly road cycling?
A: While most road cycling routes are moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, a 17-kilometer greenway, is an excellent accessible option for general cycling and families. It's closely linked to the region's mining history and winds through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or to stay overnight for cyclists?
A: El Granado and nearby towns like Puebla de Guzmán and Villanueva de los Castillejos offer local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options where cyclists can refuel and rest. These towns are often integrated into the cycling routes, providing convenient stops.
tips:
The Hermitage of Our Lady of La Peña in Puebla de Guzmán (Huelva) is one of the most emblematic sanctuaries in the Andévalo region of Huelva province, with a deep spiritual, artistic, and cultural tradition.

Location: On Cerro del Águila (Eagle Hill), about 4 km from the town center.
Origin: Probably built in the 16th century, with significant renovations in the 17th century, 1960, and 1992.
Founding legend: According to tradition, the Virgin Mary appeared to the shepherd Alonso Gómez in the 15th century, who was instructed to build a hermitage on that site.
The Zahurdón mill is located at the end of El Santo street, in Villanueva de los Castillejos. Typical old mill and a pleasant landscape with a picnic area and a nearby park.

Picturesque and quiet town in the interior of Huelva with typical white houses. Among its places of interest is the Solana windmill, the Church of Santa Catalina, the Monument to the Smuggler and a few kilometers away the well-known Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of La Peña of Puebla de Guzmán
